top_signals.title: Iran Reconnects to the Internet After 88 Days in Digital Darkness
top_signals.summary: For 88 days, millions of Iranians lived in digital darkness after the authorities imposed a nationwide Internet shutdown following the outbreak of war with the United States and Israel. But the end of one of the world’s longest-ever Internet blackouts offered scant consolation for many Iranians who reconnected to the same heavily filtered and state-controlled network after nearly three months. Still, some Iranians were relieved to escape the near-complete isolation forced on the Middle Eastern country of some 90 million people. “The…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Russia Sold Yuan Bonds After Putin’s Visit to Beijing
top_signals.summary: Russia sold its second issue of yuan-denominated sovereign bonds after President Vladimir Putin held talks in Beijing last week.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Washington nearing deal to extend Iran ceasefire, US officials say
top_signals.summary: Trump administration’s hopes that an agreement is imminent come after both sides exchanged fire
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Lilly's One-Time Cholesterol Treatment
top_signals.summary:
Eli Lilly is developing a groundbreaking one-time gene-editing treatment that could permanently lower bad cholesterol and reduce heart disease risk. Bloomberg's Madison Muller joins Open Interest to break down the science, the risks, and why Lilly is racing to expand beyond obesity drugs into its next major growth era.

(Correction: The headline of this video has been edited to clarify the treatment) (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Kalshi’s turn: Prediction markets platform sues Minnesota over law going into effect in August
top_signals.summary: Following the CFTC's suit, Kalshi has sued the state of Minnesota after Governor Tim Walz signed a law blocking prediction markets.
